title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLIII. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old woman who lived in a shoe,
        She had so many children she didn't know what to do;
        She gave them some broth without any bread,
        She whipped them all well and put them to bed.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CCLIII. Gaffers And Gammers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
